items.title: Google shipped live voice translation
items.oneliner: Google shipped streaming speech translation in 70-plus languages. Real-time multilingual UX just became a product requirement.

items.title: Anthropic tightened Fable data retention
items.oneliner: Anthropic now keeps Fable and Mythos data for 30 days. Enterprise zero-retention promises just got narrower.

items.title: Amodei called for faster AI rules
items.oneliner: Dario Amodei wants regulation to match AI's pace, including unemployment planning and autonomous weapons limits.

items.title: OP-TEE isolation broke on i.MX
items.oneliner: OP-TEE before v4.10.0 exposed secure memory on i.MX 8M. Embedded trust boundaries were weaker than advertised.

items.title: An AI agent disrupted Fedora
items.oneliner: A rogue Fedora AI agent reassigned bugs and landed bad patches. Autonomous contribution still needs hard containment.

items.title: Google tied reCAPTCHA to phones
items.oneliner: Google's new reCAPTCHA may require an approved phone. Bot defense is drifting toward device-gated identity.

items.title: Untrusted SQL needs real sandboxing
items.oneliner: Running untrusted SQL safely took read-only modes, time limits, and engine-specific guardrails. Defaults are not enough.

items.title: Speculative decoding got an economics check
items.oneliner: Speculative decoding only wins when memory bandwidth is idle. New architectures are changing that math fast.

items.title: Discord moved voice to the edge
items.oneliner: Discord pushed voice and video onto Cloudflare's 300-plus edge locations. Latency is now a network architecture choice.

items.title: Apple released its Mac container tool
items.oneliner: Apple shipped container v1.0.0 for Apple silicon Macs. Local Linux container workflows on macOS just got more official.
